item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article has been replaced with the following image, which is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0):

Source: Flickr
License: CC BY-SA 2.0
Title: The cardboard robot (named John) that Ellen and her friends made at nursery…
Author: jimjarmo
Release year: 2009

item.title: Doesn't fit in with the rest of Wondertainment
item.content:encoded: While I do enjoy this SCP. I feel it's too dark for Wondertainment. I can't see the group enslaving a sentient being. Neutral vote.

item.title: image source
item.content:encoded:
This is an instance of "kokino roboto", a cut-cardboard toy designed by Joe Freedman for Sarabande Toys.

http://www.sarabande.com/pages_2007/web_a1quality.html

https://web.archive.org/web/20160425133045/http://www.heeza.fr/en/games-toys-mecanical-games/1122-toy-joe-freedman-kokino-roboto.html

Whether this exact image was taken by someone who released it Creative Commons, I'm not sure. Joe Freedman himself can be contacted to ask for permission (or rather, Sarabande makes an e-mail available, I don't know if it's still valid).

item.title: Tearjerkers galore
item.content:encoded:
Tats released a new video. SCP-1508 was ranked #9. The first link is the full entry, the second just adds a whole new level of tearjerker to the SCP as a whole. just look at it!(the second)
https://youtu.be/ualKUR7c8E8?t=24m
https://youtu.be/ualKUR7c8E8?t=27m24s

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at anytime.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Gee wiz, Doctor Wondertainment, how come you get to have three robot skips? In all seriousness, it seems reasonable that the little Dr. W note in this article might mention General Beep and ROBO-DUDE. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

HEY KIDS!

Does doing chores turn into a total drag?

Well, my friends, now comes a new friend from DOCTOR WONDERTAINMENT©! His name is Mikey, the Chore Buddy©! He can do anything Mom and Dad want, so you can get back to having fun with your friends!

And while you're out there, check out the new 2003 WONDER-TASTIC CATA-TAINMENT-LOGUE©! Featuring our brand new toys, it's the best way to use up the free time you get from chores!

Batteries not included. If your Mikey, the Chore Buddy© begins to act out, refuse to work, writing funny things, or just plain gets a bad attitude, send him back to us for a refurbishment! Just pay shipping and handling.

To this:

HEY KIDS!

Does doing chores turn into a total drag?

Well, my friends, now comes a new friend from DOCTOR WONDERTAINMENT©, creator of General Beep and ROBO-DUDE! His name is Mikey, the Chore Buddy©! He can do anything Mom and Dad want, so you can get back to having fun with your friends!

And while you're out there, check out the new 2003 WONDER-TASTIC CATA-TAINMENT-LOGUE©! Featuring our brand new toys, it's the best way to use up the free time you get from chores!

Batteries not included. If your Mikey, the Chore Buddy© begins to act out, refuse to work, writing funny things, or just plain gets a bad attitude, send him back to us for a refurbishment! Just pay shipping and handling.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really, really like this one. The thing tidying up it's own containment chamber is a nice touch. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now this is pretty sad, and a bit too dark for Wondertainment, imho. While I know that sometimes Wondertainment's toys go haywire ( 1194) because they get damaged, I don't like the notion he'd make a toy that knows it's a slave.

Neverthless, it was a nice read. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now what am I going to do with all this laundry?
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Do like.

A bit disturbing, the implications re: Wondertainments methods, though.

+1

item.title: Re: Depression, I has it.
item.content:encoded: mmm. Death may be just as bad, but at least it'll be different, right?

item.title: Depression, I has it.
item.content:encoded:
When you're in a certain state of mind, any release, even death, seems like a blessing. I can certainly see that extending to forced servitude of some sort.

A "fate worse than death", after all, may have no ending, and nothing was meant to last eternally.

Rest well, little robot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My interpretation is that there was some kind of creature living inside the cardboard body that made it work, but now it's free and has left the body Dr. Wondertainment bound it into. So all that's left in the labs is a bit of cardboard and plastic-whatever made it work is roaming free now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the detail of him shutting down when his writing starts getting too clear - like Dr. W wanted it to shut down if it started actually thinking.

Wondertainment's maybe my favorite GOI, and this fits right in.

Yo uhave my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm flattered.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm reminded of SCP-1192.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My feels were moved, even if it was just a tiny bit. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know? I rescind my previous comment. I missed two crucial pieces of information here, and they complete the narrative. I won't say I was kicked in the feels, but I do appreciate an automaton horrified by its very nature.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Rather well done. You managed to make me feel sorry for a little cardboard robot. The idea of the enslaved soul is a well executed concept. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And thus my upvote has been released.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I interpreted it as being upset over what it was. Epecially bits like seeing its horrified reaction to its own body's transformations make me suspect that this guy may have once been human…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I enjoyed it. It wasn't about the punchline at the end, which I am neutral over, but the writing before shutdown (don't get TOO sentient now!) and the single minded helpfulness combined with the mini soul were neat to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Feels too forced for me. Sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neutral-voted for reasons I gave earlier; we already have a helpful Dr. Wondertainment robot, and even though Mikey is cute I just don't think he's different enough from Robo-Buddy,

even though...
it's really fun to see a Wario Ware reference as a skip

I mean, seriously, who needs to do Dr. Crygor Wondertainment's stupid chores when you could be a KARAOKE MACHINE like Mike from Wario Ware?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want one of these.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I give you credit for surprising my feels right in the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't care for GOIs in general, and Dr. Wondertainment in particular. That's a personal thing that doesn't really reflect on the author. I do think that the "sad skip offs itself in the service of feels" angle is way too overtly manipulative. -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. damn, now the only thing that's returned are the feels, with a vengence
2.It's all about

This little thing
headcanon

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're assuming that it understands death. It may not have realized what it did was permanent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: lmao and thus all my emotional feelings for this guy were gone

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You gave this a lot more depth, which made that final note much more impactful. Take this +1, please.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cardboard golem. Neat.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like you could've played the hook a lot better, but it's still decently written, so lightly upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But…I think there's a catch…
In that final note the robot writes

So long for now

Which puts me in anticipation for The Return Of Chore Buddy!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it was tired. Tired of having to do what it was made to do, and literally tired. Being restrained and made to not do work was, I guess, something that appealed to it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So why did the worker robot kill itself? (??)

There's a narrative in here that could be compelling, but we don't quite have enough parts to it yet. Also this needs a good bit of editing for word agreement.
